rationale: The citing paper uses Isaac, Brough, and Grayson (2016) for more than background listing: it is cited for the specific idea that expectations in rank-related marketing communications affect consumer evaluations. The paper also explicitly adapts a dependent-variable scale from Isaac et al. (2016) in its experiments, indicating methodological borrowing. While the new article develops a different mechanism for dynamic rank changes, the cited study functions as prior conceptual and empirical groundwork within ranked-list research.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Isaac et al. (2016) as part of its core conceptual base on ranked-list judgments and expectation effects, and it reuses an adapted measurement scale from that study. This makes the cited paper a supporting foundation rather than a mere passing reference.
